page: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-96 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the n1 server family. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical Intel Xeon cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2.0 threads per core. The system is equipped with 360.0 GB of RAM, translating to 3.75 GB of memory per core. The underlying x86_64 architecture includes a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 38 MB L3 cache. This configuration does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 25%, reflecting the older CPU generation. However, multi-core performance is robust, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU extended instructions ranking in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is a key strength, achieving 3918.64 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 213.36 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Redis SET operations are highly efficient, reaching 62.03 million operations per second.

The primary tradeoff of the n1-standard-96 is its weak single-threaded execution and lower efficiency in text generation tasks, as shown by weak LLM text generation benchmarks. Qualitatively, it offers a dense allocation of vCPUs and memory, making it cost-effective for highly parallelized legacy workloads rather than single-threaded applications. It is well-suited for multi-threaded database operations, high-throughput caching systems like Redis, static web serving, and parallel software compilation.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-96 is a general-purpose server from the n1 family, configured with 96 vCPUs and 360.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 48 physical cores and hyper-threading, it lacks bundled local storage and GPUs. While single-core performance and text processing benchmarks are weak, the server excels in multi-core and memory-bound operations. It delivers strong multi-core stress-ng performance, high memory read/write bandwidth, and exceptional Redis SET throughput of 62.03 million operations per second. This profile makes it a qualitative fit for highly parallelized workloads, multi-threaded database operations, and memory-intensive caching, though it presents a tradeoff in single-threaded efficiency.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-96 is an x86_64 server with 96 vCPUs and 360 GB RAM. It offers strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, making it suitable for highly parallelized database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-96 features 96 vCPUs and 360 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for parallel database workloads.
tagline: A high-vCPU general-purpose instance delivering strong multi-core throughput and high memory bandwidth for parallelized database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 360 GB RAM on Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture
Strong multi-core performance and high memory read/write bandwidth
Excellent throughput for Redis SET operations at 62.03 million ops/sec
Weak single-core and single-threaded execution performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for multi-threaded databases, caching, and parallel processing
